exploration_log.aha_moment: The catch clause only catches IllegalArgumentException, but from() calls checkNotNull which throws NPE. Adding a null check at the top of isValid is the minimal change that makes isValid safe.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could also widen the catch to include NullPointerException, but that would be a worse design. The early-return approach is cleaner.

alternatives: Catch NullPointerException in addition to IllegalArgumentException
Add null check before calling from()
key_decisions.decision: Add explicit null check at the start of isValid that returns false
key_decisions.reasoning: An early null check is the clearest and most idiomatic pattern; widening the catch clause would mask programming errors inside from()

solve_notes: Added an explicit `if (name == null) { return false; }` check at the start of `isValid()` in InternetDomainName.java. The existing catch only catches `IllegalArgumentException`; `from()` calls `checkNotNull` which throws `NullPointerException`, escaping the catch. The early-return pattern is cleaner than widening the catch clause.
task_text:
Fix InternetDomainName.isValid not handling null input gracefully

`InternetDomainName.isValid(String name)` is documented as a safe
guard method, but passing `null` causes it to throw
`NullPointerException` instead of returning `false`. The method calls
`from(name)` which calls `checkNotNull(domain)`, throwing NPE. The
try/catch in `isValid()` only catches `IllegalArgumentException`, so
the NPE escapes to the caller. Fix `isValid()` in
`InternetDomainName.java` to return `false` for a `null` argument,
making it safe to use as a guard without a prior null check.

thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: from() calls checkNotNull(domain) which is what causes the NPE; seeing this confirms the fix should be in isValid not in from()

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is only the edited method. The from() method is added to thrash_preventing because an AI agent would read it to confirm that the NPE source is checkNotNull inside from(), which validates the decision to add a null check in isValid rather than modifying from() or widening the catch clause.
